title: Sun-dried Tomato & Herby Yoghurt Lentil Bowls
summary: The herby yoghurt dressing and salted cashews make these lentil bowls feel a bit luxurious. It’s the perfect thing to make when you’ve got your friends over.

ingredients.label: 3 x 250g Pouches of Pre-Cooked Puy Lentils
ingredients.name: Cooked Puy Lentils

ingredients.label: 3 x 250g Pouches of Pre-Cooked Puy Lentils
ingredients.name: Lentil

method.description:
Pour the boiling water from the kettle into a saucepan over
a medium heat and bring back to the boil. Add the eggs and
set a timer for 61⁄2 minutes (for soft-boiled).

Meanwhile, put the garlic clove and most of the parsley and basil,
stalks and all, into a mini food processor. Blitz until finely chopped.
Add the yogurt and blitz briefly to combine. Season the yogurt
with salt and black pepper to taste.

Get a large frying pan over a high heat. Tip in the sun-dried
tomatoes with their oil and the bag of spinach. Squeeze the
unopened lentil pouches with your hands to break up the lentils,
then open and tip them into the pan. Add a splash of hot water
from the kettle. Season with salt and black pepper, stir and leave
to heat through.

Drain the eggs into a sieve when cooked and run under cold
water until cool enough to handle, then peel and halve. Cut the
lemon into four wedges and roughly chop the cashews.

Spoon the lentils into four bowls. Drizzle over the herby yogurt,
then top each bowl with a halved soft-boiled egg. Tear over the
remaining herbs and scatter over the chopped cashews. Season
with salt and black pepper and serve with the lemon wedges.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
